reasoning: The Lean signature states exactly the informal claim. `x : ℕ → Π a, f a` is a sequence in the product space; with `[Π a, TopologicalSpace (f a)]`, `𝓝 y` automatically uses `Pi.topologicalSpace` (the standard product topology, defined as the infimum of topologies induced by the projections). `Tendsto x atTop (𝓝 y)` expresses convergence of the sequence to `y`, and `∀ i, Tendsto (λ j => (x j) i) atTop (𝓝 (y i))` expresses convergence of every coordinate/projection sequence. Both directions of the iff are present, matching "if and only if". No extra or missing hypotheses; this is essentially Mathlib's `tendsto_pi_nhds` at the filter `atTop` on `ℕ`.
